source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the etcd service file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure that the etcd service file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure	that	the	/usr/lib/systemd/system/etcd.service	file	ownership	is	set	to root:root.
input_row.rationale: The	etcd	service	file	/usr/lib/systemd/system/etcd.service	controls	various parameters	that	set	the	behavior	of	the	etcd	service	in	the	master	node.	etcd	is	a	highly- available	key-value	store	which	Kubernetes	uses	for	persistent	storage	of	all	of	its	REST	API object.	You	should	set	its	file	ownership	to	maintain	the	integrity	of	the	file.	The	file	should be	owned	by	root:root. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run	the	below	command	(based	on	the	file	location	on	your	system)	on	the	master	node. For	example, stat -c %U:%G /usr/lib/systemd/system/etcd.service Verify	that	the	ownership	is	set	to	root:root.
input_row.remediation: Run	the	below	command	(based	on	the	file	location	on	your	system)	on	the	master	node. For	example, chown root:root /usr/lib/systemd/system/etcd.service Default	Value: By	default,	/usr/lib/systemd/system/etcd.service	file	ownership	is	set	to	root:root. References: 30	|	P a g e 1. https://coreos.com/etcd 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/etcd/ Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 5.1	Minimize	And	Sparingly	Use	Administrative	Privileges Minimize	administrative	privileges	and	only	use	administrative	accounts	when	they	are required.	Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 31	|	P a g e

CIS	Controls: Version	6 5.1	Minimize	And	Sparingly	Use	Administrative	Privileges Minimize	administrative	privileges	and	only	use	administrative	accounts	when	they	are required.	Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 31	|	P a g e
input_row.justification: This control requires verification of the file ownership of `/usr/lib/systemd/system/etcd.service` on the underlying host OS of Kubernetes master nodes. Alibaba Cloud APIs, CLI, and managed ACK services do not expose host-level file system details or allow direct inspection or modification of system file ownership. Both AI opinions agree that direct access is necessary, and the control documentation explicitly states it cannot be audited or modified in ACK managed clusters.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. SSH into the master node of your Kubernetes cluster where the etcd service is running. 2. Run the following command to check the ownership of the etcd service file: ``` stat -c %U:%G /usr/lib/systemd/system/etcd.service ``` 3. Verify that the output is `root:root`, confirming the file is owned by the root user and group. 4. If the ownership is not `root:root`, correct it with: ``` chown root:root /usr/lib/systemd/system/etcd.service ``` 5. Re-run the stat command to confirm the ownership is now set to `root:root`.
input_row.default_value: By default, /usr/lib/systemd/system/etcd.service file ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.additional_information: This control cannot be audited and modified in ACK managed clusters.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The rationale and the control documentation explicitly state that this control cannot be audited or modified in ACK managed clusters. It requires verification of the file ownership of `/usr/lib/systemd/system/etcd.service` on the underlying host OS of Kubernetes master nodes. Since Alibaba Cloud APIs and Aliyun CLI do not expose host-level file system details or allow direct inspection or modification of system file ownership, this control requires SSH access for verification. Therefore, the decision is to approach this audit manually.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into the master node of your Kubernetes cluster where the etcd service is running. 
2. Run the following command to check the ownership of the etcd service file: 
   ```bash
   stat -c %U:%G /usr/lib/systemd/system/etcd.service 
   ```
3. Verify that the output is `root:root`, confirming the file is owned by the root user and group.
4. If the ownership is not `root:root`, correct it with:
   ```bash
   chown root:root /usr/lib/systemd/system/etcd.service 
   ```
5. Re-run the stat command to confirm the ownership is now set to `root:root`.
